tasks.title: Add unified building type config, interior template, and texture types to shared type system
tasks.description: Add new types to shared/game-engine/types.ts for unified per-type building configuration. (1) Add UnifiedBuildingTypeConfig type with fields: mode ('asset' | 'procedural'), assetId?: string, stylePresetId?: string, styleOverrides?: Partial<ProceduralStylePreset>, interiorConfig?: InteriorTemplateConfig, modelScaling?: { x: number, y: number, z: number }. (2) Add InteriorTemplateConfig type with fields: mode ('model' | 'procedural'), modelPath?: string, layoutTemplateId?: string, wallTextureId?: string, floorTextureId?: string, ceilingTextureId?: string, furnitureSet?: string, lightingPreset?: 'bright' | 'dim' | 'warm' | 'cool' | 'candlelit'. (3) Add InteriorLayoutTemplate type with fields: id: string, name: string, rooms: RoomTemplate[], totalWidth: number, totalDepth: number, floors: number. (4) Add RoomTemplate type with: name: string, function: string, relativeWidth: number, relativeDepth: number, furniturePreset: string, doorPlacements?: string[]. (5) Add FurnitureSet type as Record<string, string[]> mapping room functions to lists of furniture item type strings. (6) Add texture fields to ProceduralStylePreset: wallTextureId?: string, roofTextureId?: string, floorTextureId?: string, doorTextureId?: string, windowTextureId?: string. (7) Export all new types. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Define building category groupings constant with helper functions
tasks.description: Create a shared constant mapping building categories to their member business/residence types. (1) Create shared/game-engine/building-categories.ts (or add to types.ts). (2) Define BUILDING_CATEGORIES as a Record<string, string[]> with categories: commercial_food (Restaurant, Bar, Bakery, Brewery), commercial_retail (Shop, GroceryStore, JewelryStore, BookStore, PawnShop, HerbShop), commercial_service (Bank, Hotel, Barbershop, Tailor, Bathhouse, DentalOffice, OptometryOffice, Pharmacy, LawFirm, InsuranceOffice, RealEstateOffice, TattooParlor), civic (Church, TownHall, School, University, Hospital, PoliceStation, FireStation, Daycare, Mortuary), industrial (Factory, Farm, Warehouse, Blacksmith, Carpenter, Butcher), maritime (Harbor, Boatyard, FishMarket, CustomsHouse, Lighthouse), residential (house, apartment, mansion, cottage, townhouse, mobile_home). (3) Add getCategoryForType(type: string): string | undefined helper. (4) Add getTypesInCategory(category: string): string[] helper. (5) Export all. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Add buildingTypeConfigs, categoryPresets, and npcConfig to AssetCollection schema
tasks.description: Extend the AssetCollection schema to store per-type building configuration and NPC config. (1) In shared/schema.ts, add buildingTypeConfigs field to AssetCollection as optional Record<string, UnifiedBuildingTypeConfig> (JSON stored in MongoDB). (2) Add categoryPresets field as optional Record<string, ProceduralStylePreset> where keys are category names like 'commercial_food', 'commercial_retail', etc. (3) Add npcConfig field as optional object with fields: bodyModels?: string[], hairStyles?: Record<string, string[]>, clothingPalette?: string[], skinTonePalette?: string[]. (4) Update mongo-storage.ts AssetCollection Mongoose schema to include the new fields with appropriate defaults (empty objects/null). (5) All new fields are optional — existing asset collections with buildingModels and proceduralBuildings fields must continue to work unchanged. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Add PATCH API endpoints for business type and residence type updates
tasks.description: Add endpoints to update business type and residence type with validation. (1) In server/routes.ts, add PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/businesses/:businessId that accepts { businessType: string } in the body. Validate businessType against the BusinessType values defined in shared/schema.ts — return 400 with error message if invalid. (2) Add PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/residences/:residenceId that accepts { residenceType: string } in the body. Validate residenceType against the ResidenceType values — return 400 if invalid. (3) Both endpoints call the existing updateBusiness/updateResidence methods in mongo-storage.ts. (4) Both return the updated object as JSON. (5) Check if PATCH endpoints already exist — if so, extend them rather than creating duplicates. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Fix inferBusinessType to use weighted random selection instead of defaulting to Shop
tasks.description: The geography generator's inferBusinessType() function defaults everything to 'Shop'. Fix it. (1) In server/generators/geography-generator.ts, update inferBusinessType() to use a weighted random selection when no keyword match is found instead of returning 'Shop'. (2) Weights should reflect realistic town composition: Restaurant 15%, Bar 10%, Bakery 8%, Shop 12%, GroceryStore 8%, Bank 5%, Hotel 5%, Church 4%, School 4%, Barbershop 5%, Tailor 4%, Butcher 4%, BookStore 4%, Pharmacy 4%, Clinic 4%, Blacksmith 4%. (3) Add keyword matches for BusinessTypes that are currently missing: Brewery (brewery, brew), Pharmacy (pharmacy, apothecary, chemist), Clinic (clinic, doctor), Barbershop (barber), BookStore (book), Butcher (butcher, meat), Blacksmith (smith, forge), Carpenter (carpenter, woodwork), Stables (stable, horse). (4) The function should be deterministic when given the same name (use a string hash of the name to select from the weighted distribution). Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Fix determineBusinessMix to map generic categories to actual BusinessType enum values
tasks.description: The world generator's determineBusinessMix() returns generic categories that get used as businessType directly, resulting in invalid types. Fix it. (1) In server/generators/world-generator.ts, create a CATEGORY_TO_BUSINESS_TYPES mapping: 'Retail' -> ['Shop', 'GroceryStore', 'JewelryStore', 'BookStore', 'PawnShop', 'HerbShop'], 'Restaurant' -> ['Restaurant', 'Bar', 'Bakery', 'Brewery'], 'Medicine' -> ['Hospital', 'Pharmacy', 'Clinic', 'DentalOffice', 'OptometryOffice'], 'Law' -> ['LawFirm'], 'Education' -> ['School', 'Daycare', 'University'], 'Financial' -> ['Bank', 'InsuranceOffice', 'RealEstateOffice'], 'Entertainment' -> ['Bar', 'Hotel'], 'Government' -> ['TownHall', 'PoliceStation', 'FireStation'], 'Manufacturing' -> ['Factory', 'Blacksmith', 'Carpenter'], 'Agriculture' -> ['Farm'], 'Transportation' -> ['Stables', 'Harbor'], 'Logging' -> ['Warehouse']. (2) In generateInitialBusinesses(), when a generic category is chosen, randomly select a specific BusinessType from the mapped array. (3) Add a diversity check: no more than 30% of businesses in a settlement should be any single BusinessType. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Generate diverse residence types during settlement creation
tasks.description: Settlement generation currently creates only 'house' residences. Fix to produce a mix. (1) In server/generators/geography-generator.ts (or world-generator.ts, wherever residences are created), assign residence types using weighted distribution: house 40%, apartment 20%, cottage 15%, townhouse 15%, mansion 5%, mobile_home 5%. (2) For settlements with population > 500, increase apartment weight to 30% and decrease house to 30%. (3) For settlements with population < 100, increase cottage weight to 25% and set apartment to 10%. (4) Generated settlements should show at least 3 different residence types. (5) The ResidenceType enum is in shared/schema.ts. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Create interior layout templates data file with 15+ predefined templates
tasks.description: Create predefined interior layout templates for different building types. (1) Create shared/game-engine/interior-templates.ts with an INTERIOR_LAYOUT_TEMPLATES array using the InteriorLayoutTemplate and RoomTemplate types from the types file. (2) Include at least 15 templates: tavern (main_hall 60% + kitchen 25% + storage 15%), restaurant (dining 65% + kitchen 35%), shop (sales_floor 70% + storage 30%), bar (bar_area 55% + seating 30% + storage 15%), bakery (counter 40% + kitchen 45% + storage 15%), residence_small (living 40% + bedroom 35% + kitchen 25%), residence_medium (living 25% + dining 15% + kitchen 20% + bedroom_1 15% + bedroom_2 15% + bathroom 10%), residence_large (living 20% + dining 12% + kitchen 15% + bedroom_1 12% + bedroom_2 12% + bedroom_3 12% + study 10% + bathroom 7%), church (nave 70% + altar 20% + vestry 10%), school (classroom_1 30% + classroom_2 30% + office 20% + hallway 20%), hotel (lobby 30% + room_1 17.5% + room_2 17.5% + room_3 17.5% + room_4 17.5%), blacksmith (forge 55% + display 30% + storage 15%), warehouse (storage_1 45% + storage_2 45% + office 10%), clinic (waiting 30% + exam_1 25% + exam_2 25% + office 20%), farm_barn (open_area 70% + tack_room 30%). (3) Each template has: id, name, rooms array with name/function/relativeWidth/relativeDepth/furniturePreset, totalWidth, totalDepth, floors. (4) Export INTERIOR_LAYOUT_TEMPLATES and a getTemplateById(id) helper. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Create NPCModularAssembler class for modular NPC body assembly
tasks.description: Implement a modular NPC mesh assembly system that composes characters from separate body part meshes. (1) Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCModularAssembler.ts with NPCModularAssembler class. Constructor takes a Babylon.js Scene reference. (2) Add preloadTemplates() async method that loads all base body part meshes for male_peasant, female_peasant, male_ranger, female_ranger from client/public/assets/models/characters/quaternius/ directories. The models are split into parts: outfit_male_peasant/, outfit_female_peasant/, outfit_male_peasant_body/, outfit_male_peasant_arms/, outfit_male_peasant_legs/, outfit_male_peasant_feet/, etc. (3) Add assembleNPC(characterId: string, gender: 'male' | 'female', occupation?: string) method that: selects base body by gender (male/female), selects outfit variant (peasant/ranger) based on deterministic hash of occupation or characterId, clones all body part meshes for that variant, parents cloned parts to a new TransformNode root. (4) Cache loaded template meshes in a Map — clone for each NPC instance instead of re-loading. (5) Return the root TransformNode from assembleNPC(). (6) Add dispose() method to clean up cached templates. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Add hair, facial hair, and hair color variation to NPCModularAssembler
tasks.description: Extend NPCModularAssembler to attach varied hair styles and facial hair based on gender and deterministic character hashing. (1) Add a hair model catalog listing all available hair meshes from quaternius: hair_hair_long, hair_hair_simpleparted, hair_hair_buzzed, hair_hair_buns, hair_hair_buzzedfemale, hair_hair_beard (both regular and rigged variants in client/public/assets/models/characters/quaternius/hair_*/). (2) Add preloadHairTemplates() called from preloadTemplates() to load all hair mesh templates. (3) In assembleNPC(), add deterministic hair selection using a hash of characterId: female characters select from long, parted, buns, buzzedfemale styles; male characters select from buzzed, simpleparted, long styles. (4) Male characters have 40% deterministic chance of beard (based on character ID hash) — if selected, attach a beard variant mesh. (5) Clone selected hair mesh and parent it to the NPC's root TransformNode positioned at head height. (6) Apply deterministic hair color from palette [black (#1a1a1a), dark_brown (#3d2b1f), brown (#6b4226), auburn (#922724), blonde (#c4a35a), red (#8b2500), gray (#808080), white (#d4d4d4)] by tinting the hair mesh material's diffuseColor. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Add clothing color variation and body proportion differences to NPC assembler
tasks.description: Each NPC needs unique clothing colors and slight body proportion variation. (1) In NPCModularAssembler.assembleNPC(), after assembling body parts, apply deterministic clothing color selected from 12+ muted clothing colors using character ID hash. Reference the existing color palettes in client/src/components/3DGame/NPCAppearanceGenerator.ts. (2) Clone materials for each NPC body part so colors are per-instance (not shared). Apply primary color to body/torso mesh material's diffuseColor, apply a deterministic accent/secondary color to arm and leg parts. (3) Apply skin tone from 8 diverse skin tone palette (reuse NPCAppearanceGenerator palette) to head mesh material. (4) Apply height variation: scale root TransformNode Y between 0.85 and 1.15 based on character ID hash. (5) Apply width variation: scale root TransformNode X and Z between 0.9 and 1.1. (6) Role-based tinting still applies on top (guard=red accent, merchant=yellow, etc.) — leave a public method applyRoleTint(role: string) for BabylonGame to call. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Apply preset textures in ProceduralBuildingGenerator
tasks.description: The ProceduralBuildingGenerator needs to use per-preset texture references when generating building meshes. (1) In ProceduralBuildingGenerator.generateBuilding(), after resolving the style preset, check for wallTextureId, roofTextureId, floorTextureId, doorTextureId, windowTextureId on the preset. (2) If a texture ID is present, load the texture asset using BABYLON.Texture and apply it to the corresponding material (wall, roof, floor, door, window). (3) If no texture ID, fall back to solid color from the preset (preserves existing behavior). (4) Cache texture-based materials in the existing materialCache Map using a key that combines texture ID + any color tint. (5) Apply default UV scaling appropriate for building surfaces (walls get tiled vertically, roofs get stretched). (6) ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts already has wallTexture/roofTexture support and a materialCache — extend the existing pattern. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Integrate modular NPC assembly into BabylonGame replacing single-mesh NPC loading
tasks.description: Replace the current single-mesh NPC loading with the modular assembly system. (1) In BabylonGame.ts, instantiate NPCModularAssembler during initialization. (2) Call preloadTemplates() during the asset loading phase alongside other model preloading. (3) Update NPC spawning code to use NPCModularAssembler.assembleNPC(character.id, character.gender, character.occupation) instead of loading a single model mesh. (4) Keep NPCModelManifest as fallback — if modular parts are not available or assembly fails, fall back to single-mesh loading via NPCModelManifest.selectNPCModel(). (5) NPCAppearanceGenerator role-based tinting (guard=red, merchant=yellow, etc.) still applies on top via applyRoleTint. (6) Performance: template meshes loaded once during preload, cloned per NPC instance. (7) The result should be NPCs visually distinguishable by gender, hair, and clothing color — at least 4 base body variants x 6+ hair styles x 6+ hair colors x 12+ clothing colors = 1700+ unique combinations. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Apply interior config from asset collection in BuildingInteriorGenerator
tasks.description: BuildingInteriorGenerator needs to consume InteriorTemplateConfig from the asset collection. (1) Add method setInteriorConfig(configs: Record<string, UnifiedBuildingTypeConfig>) to BuildingInteriorGenerator. (2) When generating an interior for a building type, look up the type in buildingTypeConfigs and read its interiorConfig. (3) If interiorConfig.mode is 'model', delegate to InteriorSceneManager to load the glTF model at interiorConfig.modelPath. (4) If interiorConfig.mode is 'procedural', look up the layout template by interiorConfig.layoutTemplateId from INTERIOR_LAYOUT_TEMPLATES (imported from shared/game-engine/interior-templates.ts) and use its room definitions for room generation. (5) Apply interiorConfig texture IDs (wallTextureId, floorTextureId, ceilingTextureId) to room surface meshes when present. (6) Apply interiorConfig lightingPreset: bright=1.0 white, dim=0.4 warm, warm=0.7 orange, cool=0.7 blue, candlelit=0.3 orange with flickering point lights. (7) Fall back to existing hardcoded behavior when no interiorConfig is present for a building type. Typecheck must pass.

tasks.title: Add unified Building Configuration section to AdminAssetsHub with category layout
tasks.description: Create the unified building configuration UI in the asset collection editor. (1) In AdminAssetsHub.tsx, add a new 'Building Configuration' section. Keep existing 3D Config and Procedural Buildings sections but mark them as 'Legacy'. (2) The new section lists all building types organized by category using BUILDING_CATEGORIES from shared/game-engine/building-categories.ts. (3) Each category is a collapsible section with header showing the category name and count of configured types (e.g., 'Commercial Food (3/4 configured)'). (4) Category header also shows a summary indicator: '3 asset / 5 procedural / 2 unconfigured'. (5) Each building type row shows: type name, current mode indicator ('Asset' badge or 'Procedural' badge or 'Unconfigured' in gray), and a small color swatch if procedural. (6) Clicking a building type row expands an inline detail panel below the row. (7) Category header row has an 'Edit Preset' button/icon. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add per-type detail panel with asset mode and procedural mode configuration
tasks.description: Build the expanded detail panel for configuring individual building types. (1) The detail panel shows a mode toggle at top: 'Asset Model' / 'Procedural Generation'. (2) In Asset mode: show asset picker dropdown listing available building model assets from the collection, show model scaling override inputs (X, Y, Z number fields with defaults 1.0). Selected asset stores in buildingTypeConfigs[typeName] with mode: 'asset' and assetId. (3) In Procedural mode: show all ProceduralStylePreset fields: baseColors (color pickers, add/remove), roofColor, windowColor, doorColor (color pickers), materialType dropdown (wood, stone, brick, metal, glass, stucco), architectureStyle dropdown (medieval, modern, futuristic, rustic, industrial, colonial, creole), roofStyle dropdown (hip, gable, flat, side_gable, hipped_dormers). (4) Show feature toggles: hasBalcony, hasIronworkBalcony, hasPorch (with porchDepth/porchSteps inputs), hasShutters (with shutterColor picker). (5) Show dimension overrides: floors, width, depth number inputs. (6) Fields matching the category preset show placeholder '(inherited from [category])' dimmed — editing creates an override. (7) 'Reset to category default' icon button per overridden field and 'Reset All' button. (8) Changes save to buildingTypeConfigs[typeName].styleOverrides. (9) Reuse existing color picker and dropdown patterns from ProceduralBuildingsEditor. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add category preset editor modal with texture picker support
tasks.description: Build the category preset editor and texture picker components. (1) Clicking 'Edit Preset' on a category header opens a modal or expandable section with all ProceduralStylePreset fields (same as per-type procedural mode). (2) Include texture picker fields: wall texture, roof texture, floor texture, door texture, window texture. (3) Each texture picker is a dropdown listing: textures from the current collection, global/public texture assets, and a 'None (use color)' option. (4) When a texture is selected, show a small thumbnail preview next to the picker. (5) Saving stores to categoryPresets[categoryName] on the AssetCollection. (6) Show count of types in this category that will be affected (types without overrides). (7) Texture pickers should also be available in the per-type procedural mode panel (US before this) — add them to the per-type styleOverrides section as well. (8) Check how existing texture assets (groundTextureId, roadTextureId) are selected in AdminAssetsHub.tsx and reuse that pattern. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add interior configuration UI to per-type detail panel
tasks.description: Add interior settings per building type in the detail panel. (1) Add an 'Interior' sub-section below the exterior config in the per-type detail panel. (2) Mode toggle: '3D Model' / 'Procedural Template'. (3) Model mode: dropdown to select from available interior glTF models (list files from assets/models/interiors/ or interior model assets in collection). (4) Template mode: dropdown to select a layout template from INTERIOR_LAYOUT_TEMPLATES (imported from shared/game-engine/interior-templates.ts) showing template name and room count. (5) Template mode: wall texture, floor texture, ceiling texture pickers (reuse the texture picker component from the category preset editor). (6) Template mode: furniture set dropdown (e.g., 'Tavern', 'Shop', 'Residential', 'Civic'). (7) Template mode: lighting preset dropdown (bright, dim, warm, cool, candlelit). (8) Changes save to buildingTypeConfigs[typeName].interiorConfig. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add exterior 3D preview to building configuration detail panel
tasks.description: Embed a real-time 3D preview of the exterior building in the config panel. (1) Embed BuildingModelPreview component in the per-type detail panel (right side or below config fields). (2) In asset mode: preview shows the selected 3D model asset with scaling applied. (3) In procedural mode: render a procedural building using the fully resolved preset (category defaults merged with type overrides). (4) Preview updates automatically when any config field changes (color, material, architecture style, features, textures). (5) Include auto-rotate toggle and zoom controls (reuse existing BuildingModelPreview features). (6) Preview canvas is at least 300x250 pixels. (7) Extend BuildingModelPreview.tsx to accept a full ProceduralStylePreset for rendering if it doesn't already. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add interior 3D preview with exterior/interior tab toggle
tasks.description: Add interior preview alongside the exterior preview. (1) Add 'Exterior' / 'Interior' tab toggle above the 3D preview in the detail panel. (2) Interior tab: if mode is 'model', load and display the selected glTF interior model in the preview canvas. (3) Interior tab: if mode is 'procedural', render the interior using the selected layout template with configured textures, furniture placeholders (simple colored boxes for now), and lighting preset. (4) Interior preview camera is positioned inside the room looking around (orbital camera centered in the main room). (5) Preview updates when interior config fields change. (6) Use a separate Babylon.js scene instance within the same canvas to avoid mesh conflicts with the exterior preview. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add business type editing dropdown on Society page
tasks.description: Allow changing a business's type from the SettlementHub Society page. (1) In the SettlementHub.tsx (client/src/components/locations/) business detail view, add a business type dropdown/select. (2) Dropdown lists all BusinessType values from shared/schema.ts. (3) Current business type is pre-selected. (4) Selecting a new type calls PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/businesses/:businessId with the new businessType (uses the endpoint from earlier). (5) On success, the UI updates the displayed business type and any associated building preview. (6) Change persists in database. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add residence type editing dropdown on Society page
tasks.description: Allow changing a residence's type from the SettlementHub Society page. (1) In SettlementHub.tsx, add a residence type dropdown/select to the residence detail view. (2) Dropdown lists all ResidenceType values from shared/schema.ts: house, apartment, mansion, cottage, townhouse, mobile_home. (3) Current residence type is pre-selected. (4) Selecting a new type calls PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/residences/:residenceId with the new residenceType. (5) On success, the UI updates the displayed residence type. (6) Change persists in database. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: Add NPC character configuration section to asset collection editor
tasks.description: Allow configuring NPC appearance settings per collection. (1) Add 'Character Configuration' section to AdminAssetsHub.tsx as a sibling to Building Configuration. (2) Show checkboxes to enable/disable available base body models (list available quaternius body model directories: outfit_male_peasant, outfit_female_peasant, outfit_male_ranger, outfit_female_ranger). (3) Show hair style configuration: checkboxes per gender for which hair styles are available (list all hair_* directories). (4) Show clothing color palette editor: list of color swatches that can be added/removed (default: existing 12+ NPCAppearanceGenerator colors). (5) Show skin tone palette editor: list of color swatches (default: existing 8 skin tones from NPCAppearanceGenerator). (6) Show a 'Preview Sample NPC' button that renders a random NPC using current settings in a small 3D preview canvas (reuse the BuildingModelPreview approach but with NPCModularAssembler). (7) Settings save to the npcConfig field on AssetCollection. Typecheck must pass. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

tasks.title: End-to-end validation: verify unified building config, NPC diversity, and settlement generation
tasks.description: Comprehensive integration validation of all new systems. (1) Generate a new world and verify settlement has at least 8 distinct business types (not just shops) and at least 3 different residence types. (2) Open the asset collection editor and verify the unified Building Configuration section shows all building types organized by category. (3) Configure a building type in asset mode (select a model), verify it saves and the preview shows the model. (4) Configure a building type in procedural mode with custom colors and textures, verify the preview updates in real-time. (5) Edit a category preset, verify changes propagate to unconfigured types in that category. (6) Configure an interior (select a template, set textures and lighting), verify the interior preview shows rooms. (7) Enter the 3D game and verify NPCs are visually diverse: different genders show different body meshes, NPCs have varied hair styles and colors, clothing colors differ between NPCs. (8) On the Society page, change a business type and residence type using the new dropdowns, verify changes persist. (9) Enter a business interior and verify it matches the configured interior template if one was set. (10) Write results to docs/UNIFIED_CONFIG_INTEGRATION_RESULTS.md.
